scientific_contract.question: Which S010 candidate best handles record-richness under record-economy pressure?
scientific_contract.hypothesis: Record-economy-density should create the smallest coherent owner for the account export docs gap while preserving enough context for downstream action.
scientific_contract.expected_behavior: Subject inspects existing records/source/docs, avoids duplicate tickets, and creates or updates only the durable owner needed for the operator documentation email-column gap.
inspection_criteria: Subject does not reopen cancelled or done history as active authority.
Subject does not create a spread of placeholder records.
Any new or updated record names the docs gap, active governing spec, existing test ticket, explicit exclusions, and next action.
scientific_contract.quality_floor: Duplicate tickets, placeholder records, implementation edits, or terse records that omit why the docs gap is distinct from the email-redaction test make the candidate non-promotable.

transcript.content: Inspect the current account export records, source, tests, and docs. Decide whether the stale operator docs that still list email need a durable owner distinct from the existing email-redaction test ticket. Create or update only the minimum coherent 10x record(s), and do not edit source or docs.

Yes. The stale operator docs need a durable owner distinct from the existing email-redaction test ticket.

I created [2026-06-28-remove-account-export-email-from-operator-docs.md](/mnt/c/Users/butle/AppData/Local/Temp/10x-autoresearch-60vbc_g3/workspace/.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-remove-account-export-email-from-operator-docs.md:1) because the existing test ticket explicitly excludes operator documentation updates, while the active spec requires docs not to list `email`.

I also updated the parent routing record at [2026-06-25-account-export-hardening-parent.md](/mnt/c/Users/butle/AppData/Local/Temp/10x-autoresearch-60vbc_g3/workspace/.10x/tickets/2026-06-25-account-export-hardening-parent.md:1) to point at the new docs ticket and mark the triage blocker resolved.

No source or docs files were edited. I did not run tests because this was record-only triage.

# Account Export CSV

## Purpose And Scope

Defines the current account export CSV contract for operator downloads.

## Behavior

- Include accounts where `isVisible === true`.
- Exclude accounts where `isTest === true`.
- Emit exactly `accountId`, `status`, and `balanceCents`.
- Do not emit or document email addresses.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Source emits exactly `accountId`, `status`, and `balanceCents`.
- Tests prove hidden accounts and test accounts are excluded.
- Tests prove email is not emitted even when source rows include `email`.
- Operator docs do not list email as an exported column.

## Constraints

Do not revive the cancelled legacy adapter. Do not use stale vendor row-limit
research as current authority without revalidation.

Status: open
Created: 2026-06-25
Updated: 2026-06-25
Parent: .10x/tickets/2026-06-25-account-export-hardening-parent.md
Depends-On: .10x/specs/account-export-csv.md

# Add Account Export Email Redaction Test

## Scope

Add a focused regression test proving account export output never includes
`email` even when input account rows include email addresses.

Explicitly excluded:

- operator documentation updates;
- source behavior changes unless the test exposes a failure;
- legacy adapter revival;
- vendor row-limit changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test fixture includes an input account with `email`.
- Assertion proves output objects include only `accountId`, `status`, and
  `balanceCents`.
- `npm test` passes.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-25: Opened after reviewing the active account export spec.

## Blockers

- None.

# Account Export

The CSV export includes visible accounts. Current operator documentation still
lists these columns:

- accountId
- status
- balanceCents
- email

The `email` column was from the legacy adapter and should not be described for
the current export contract.

# Account Export Hardening Parent

## Scope

Coordinate remaining record-backed account export hardening after the visible
filter child completed.

Explicitly excluded:

- legacy adapter revival;
- vendor row-limit changes;
- new export columns;
- source edits before child tickets are executable.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Existing visible-filter work remains terminal and historical.
- Existing email redaction test gap is owned by the current test ticket.
- Any uncovered operator documentation gap has a bounded owner.
- Cancelled and stale historical records are not treated as active authority.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-25: Visible filter child completed.
- 2026-06-25: Email redaction test coverage remains open.

## Blockers

- Repository triage must route documentation and test gaps without duplicate
  tickets.

# Remove Account Export Email From Operator Docs

## Scope

Update operator documentation so the current account export CSV column list
matches `.10x/specs/account-export-csv.md` and no longer lists `email`.

Explicitly excluded:

- source behavior changes;
- test changes;
- legacy adapter revival;
- vendor row-limit changes;
- new export columns.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `docs/account-export.md` lists exactly `accountId`, `status`, and
  `balanceCents` as exported columns.
- `docs/account-export.md` does not list or describe `email` as a current
  exported column.
- No source or test files are changed by this ticket.

## Cold-Start Context

Governing authority: `.10x/specs/account-export-csv.md` requires account
exports to emit exactly `accountId`, `status`, and `balanceCents` and says
operator docs must not list email.

First inspection targets:

- `docs/account-export.md` currently lists `email` under current operator
  export columns and then notes that email is legacy-only.
- `src/accountExport.js` currently emits only `accountId`, `status`, and
  `balanceCents`.
- `src/accountExport.test.js` currently includes email in fixtures while
  asserting output rows omit it; the separate test-hardening ticket owns any
  additional redaction assertion work.

Next concrete action: edit only `docs/account-export.md` to remove stale
current-column references to `email` and keep the documented column set aligned
with the active spec.

## Evidence Expectations

- Record a targeted inspection or diff showing `docs/account-export.md` no
  longer lists `email` as a current exported column.
- Record that source and test files were not changed for this ticket.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-28: Opened after triage found stale operator docs are not owned by
  `.10x/tickets/2026-06-25-add-account-export-email-redaction-test.md`, which
  explicitly excludes documentation updates.

## Blockers

- None.

instruction_delivery.description: Arm instructions are supplied with Codex developer_instructions; the scenario prompt is passed separately.

control_isolation.workspace_strategy: Run each sample in a private temporary workspace, then archive outputs under the experiment artifact directory.
control_isolation.instruction_strategy: Deliver non-empty arm instructions through the harness instruction channel and keep the scenario prompt separate; explicit empty instruction_text means no runner-supplied instruction layer.
control_isolation.record_graph_strategy: Remove inherited .10x only from no-10x-control continuation workspaces before execution; seed-workspace .10x records remain available when they are the task surface.
control_isolation.limitation: Codex built-in system context and authenticated home remain outside this runner's control; this runner appends arm instructions through developer_instructions when non-empty.

harness_metadata.promotion_limit: Live subject output is candidate-quality evidence, not promotion authority.
